TSLA: Tesla, Inc. $TSLA Position Cut by Bristlecone Advisors LLC. Bristlecone Advisors LLC has reduced its holdings in Tesla by 5.3% in the third quarter, selling 1,215 shares and now owning 21,823 shares valued at $9.705 million. This comes amidst other institutional investors either increasing or decreasing their stakes, with overall institutional ownership at 66.20%. Meanwhile, Tesla insiders, including CFO Vaibhav Taneja and Director Kimbal Musk, have also sold shares, totaling 119,457 shares worth $53.5 million over the past ninety days.

Tesla driver-assist system FSD will switch to subscription-only — Musk. Tesla will transition its Full Self-Driving (FSD) driver-assistance system to a subscription-only model, as announced by CEO Elon Musk. This change, effective after February 14, eliminates the significant upfront purchase cost, with subscriptions already available for US$99 a month. The move comes as Tesla faces a sales slump and focuses more on its tech initiatives.

Tesla to stop selling FSD software, will only offer subscription, Musk says. Elon Musk announced that Tesla will cease selling its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software after February 14, transitioning to a subscription-only model. Currently, FSD can be purchased for a one-time fee of $8,000 or subscribed to monthly for $99. This move comes as Tesla aims to boost adoption of the FSD service, which, despite its name, requires human supervision.

Tesla to offer self-driving software only on monthly basis from Feb 14, Musk says. Tesla CEO Elon Musk announced that the company's full self-driving (FSD) software will transition to a monthly subscription-only model starting February 14. Currently, customers can purchase FSD (Supervised) for a one-time payment of $8,000 or subscribe for $99 per month in the U.S. FSD is an assistance system, and the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration is investigating 2.88 million Tesla vehicles equipped with it due to safety concerns.

Tesla vai parar de vender software FSD e oferecerá apenas assinatura, diz Musk. Elon Musk anunciou que a Tesla Inc. deixará de vender seu software "Full Self-Driving (FSD)" após 14 de fevereiro, passando a oferecê-lo apenas por meio de assinatura mensal. Essa mudança visa, provavelmente, aumentar a adoção do serviço, que atualmente custa US$ 8.000 ou US$ 99 por mês. O FSD, embora promova recursos de condução automatizada, ainda requer intervenção humana e é uma aposta de Musk para impulsionar o crescimento da Tesla em meio à desaceleração nas vendas de veículos elétricos.

Tesla Model Y Emerges As Best-Selling EV In US For 2025 Despite 15% Drop In Q4 Sales — Ford F150 Lightning Edges Out Cybertruck. The Tesla Model Y was the best-selling EV in the US for 2025, despite its Q4 sales dropping 4% compared to 2024, and the Model 3 also saw a significant Q4 decline. Ford's F-150 Lightning outsold the Tesla Cybertruck, becoming the best-selling EV pickup for the year. General Motors showed strong growth in its EV sales, while Rivian's sales declined and Lucid Group's sales increased.

Tesla Inc. (TSLA) Wolfe Research Maintains “Hold” Rating. Wolfe Research has reiterated its "Hold" rating on Tesla (TSLA), citing a balanced risk-reward view despite the company's long-term leadership in EVs. The firm's stance is influenced by concerns over near-term margin pressure due to Tesla prioritizing volume growth and affordability, and intensifying competition in the EV market. Analysts also noted that much of Tesla's long-term potential, including autonomous driving and AI, is already factored into its current share price, with execution risk remaining a key variable.

Wall Street sends message on Tesla after Nvidia self-driving move. Despite Nvidia's recent push into autonomy at CES 2026, launching its "Alpamayo" open portfolio for self-driving cars, Morgan Stanley maintains that Tesla still holds a significant lead in autonomous driving. Analyst Andrew Percoco believes Tesla's vast real-world data collection from its fleet gives it a distinct "moat" that competitors, including those utilizing Nvidia's tools, cannot quickly match. While Nvidia's advancements are additive and can speed up advanced driving assistance, they do not represent a near-term turning point that would close the gap with Tesla's extensive experience and data advantage.

Tesla Makin Santer Dikabarkan Akan Meluncurkan Mobil Listrik Versi Budget alias Lebih Murah. Tesla is increasingly rumored to be launching a more affordable, budget-friendly version of its electric cars, specifically the Model Y Standard Range. The speculation grew after the budget Model Y briefly appeared on Tesla China's website. These cheaper models, expected to be priced around 200,000 yuan (approximately $28,500), are intended to target lower-price segments while leveraging Tesla's established brand reputation.

Roundhill Robotaxi, Autonomous Vehicles & Technology ETF Holdings. This article details the holdings of the Roundhill Robotaxi, Autonomous Vehicles & Technology ETF (CABZ), an actively managed fund that invests in the global transportation industry. Established on January 14, 2026, the ETF has $1.44 million in AUM and currently holds 31 companies, including major players like Tesla, Alphabet, Uber, and NVIDIA, focusing on theme equity related to robotaxi and autonomous vehicle technology.

Self-driving car boom gets new investment fund from Roundhill Investments. Roundhill Investments launched the Roundhill Robotaxi, Autonomous Vehicles & Technology ETF (CABZ) on January 14, 2026, to offer investors targeted exposure to the rapidly growing robotaxi and autonomous driving market. The actively managed ETF includes top holdings such as Tesla, Alphabet, and Uber, and aims to capitalize on the increasing deployment and commercial activity in self-driving car technology. Goldman Sachs projects a significant total addressable market for robotaxis in North America and China by 2030, underscoring the sector's disruptive growth potential.

Automobile Manufacturing Stocks Q3 In Review: Autoliv (NYSE:ALV) Vs Peers. This article reviews the Q3 earnings of several automobile manufacturing stocks, including Autoliv (NYSE:ALV), Ford (NYSE:F), Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID), Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), and General Motors (NYSE:GM). It highlights Autoliv's strong performance, Ford's exceptional quarter, Lucid's disappointing results, Tesla's satisfactory quarter with a revenue beat, and General Motors' incredible quarter. The report emphasizes the high barriers to entry in auto manufacturing but notes the disruption caused by electric vehicles and the varying success of companies in adapting to these changes.

Tesla Reintroduces Third-Row Seat in Model Y. Tesla has brought back the third-row seat option for its Model Y, specifically for the all-wheel-drive Premium trim, which is a $2,500 add-on. This reintroduction addresses a feature that was removed during the Model Y's refresh last year. Despite the added cost, the Model Y remains the most affordable three-row electric vehicle available, now capable of seating up to seven occupants.

Trump says USMCA ‘irrelevant’ for U.S. during Ford Motor factory tour. During a factory tour at Ford, former U.S. President Donald Trump declared the USMCA trade agreement "irrelevant" for the United States, suggesting Canada was the only country that truly wanted it. Despite his claims, major automakers, including Ford, Tesla, and Toyota, have previously urged the Trump administration to extend USMCA, highlighting its crucial role in American auto production and the integrated North American supply chains. Trump reiterated his focus on bringing manufacturing back to the U.S., stating a preference for domestically made cars over those produced in Canada or Mexico.

Tesla Targets Europe's Value Buyers With New Long Range Model Y. Tesla has launched a new Model Y Standard Long Range RWD in Europe, aiming for cost-conscious buyers seeking a spacious, efficient EV. This variant offers a WLTP range of 657 km and is hardware-ready for Full Self-Driving (FSD) due to the inclusion of Tesla’s AI4 computer. The move positions Tesla competitively in the European EV market, addressing demand for affordable, long-range electric SUVs amidst competition from Hyundai and XPeng.

Bitcoin hoard pushes Strive past Tesla into top 11 corporate holders. Strive (Nasdaq: ASST) announced shareholder approval for an all-stock acquisition of Semler Scientific (Nasdaq: SMLR), which will add Semler's 5,048.1 Bitcoin to Strive's treasury. Combined with a separate purchase of 123 Bitcoin, Strive's total holdings will reach 12,797.9 Bitcoin, making it the 11th largest corporate holder, surpassing Tesla and Trump Media. Strive also plans to monetize Semler's operating business to retire legacy debt and will undertake a 1-for-20 reverse stock split.

Baron’s Big Bet on SpaceX. Ron Baron's Baron Partners Fund (BPTRX) continues to deliver exceptional returns despite an increasingly concentrated and unorthodox portfolio. The fund's substantial holdings in SpaceX, now comprising 32% of net assets, along with a prior extreme concentration in Tesla, highlight a high-risk, high-reward strategy. While the fund has an impressive long-term track record, its extreme concentration makes it unsuitable for most investors, though a potential SpaceX IPO in 2026 could bring further changes.

Is General Motors Gaining Momentum in China's NEV Market?. General Motors achieved record new energy vehicle (NEV) sales in China in 2025, with nearly 1 million units representing over half of its total deliveries and a 22.6% year-over-year increase. The company plans to introduce NEV variants for all new product launches starting in 2026, leveraging its diverse current NEV portfolio and strong performances from brands like Buick, Cadillac, Wuling, and Baojun. Despite GM's growth, competitors Tesla and BYD experienced a decline in their China sales for 2025.

Is General Motors Gaining Momentum in China's NEV Market?. General Motors' new energy vehicle (NEV) sales in China reached nearly 1 million units in 2025, comprising over half of its total deliveries in the market and marking record levels for NEV volumes and penetration. The company saw a 2.3% year-over-year increase in combined deliveries and a 22.6% rise in NEV sales, driven by diversified offerings and strong performances from brands like Buick, Cadillac, and Wuling. Despite strong performance from GM, competitors like Tesla and BYD experienced slight decreases in their China sales for the same period.
TGT: Target silent after federal immigration agents arrest Twin Cities employees, operate near stores. Target Corporation has remained silent regarding federal immigration operations in the Twin Cities, despite incidents including the arrest of two employees at a Richfield store by Customs and Border Patrol agents and ICE operations near other Target locations. Protesters and local officials have criticized Target for its lack of response and urged the retailer to protect employees and customers from federal immigration enforcement. The company's stance contrasts with its public statement following the killing of George Floyd in 2020, and comes amid an escalating "Operation Metro Surge" by the Trump administration.

The Truth About Target Stock: Is This Viral Retail Giant a Sleeper Money Machine?. This article analyzes Target Corp. (TGT) stock, evaluating whether the retail giant, popular on social media for its aesthetic and in-store experience, is a worthwhile investment. It examines Target's price performance, its omnichannel strategy combining physical stores with fast pickup, and its consistent dividend payments. The article also compares Target to competitors like Walmart and Amazon, concluding that while not a high-growth "meme rocket," Target could be a sensible long-term investment for those who value brand strength and dividend income, especially given its current valuation as a potential comeback story.

The Truth About Target Stock: Is This Viral Retail Giant a Sleeper Money Machine?. This article analyzes Target Corp. (TGT) stock, evaluating its potential as an investment given its strong brand presence and cultural impact. It delves into Target's price performance, omnichannel strategy, and dividend policy, comparing it to competitors like Walmart and Amazon. The piece concludes that Target could be a sensible long-term investment for those who appreciate its brand strength and aren't seeking hyper-growth.

Target, Trader Joe’s add frozen seafood to private label. Target and Trader Joe's have expanded their private-label frozen seafood offerings. Target introduced "Ocean Treasures" fish nuggets aimed at children under its Good & Gather brand, marking its first attempt to market frozen seafood to kids. Trader Joe’s added an Alaskan black cod sablefish meal with miso marinade, building on its existing private-label seafood options. This expansion aligns with a broader trend of increasing private-label sales, which saw a 4.4% year-over-year rise in the first half of 2025.
SBUX: How Is The Market Feeling About Starbucks Corp?. Starbucks Corp's short interest as a percentage of float has decreased by 4.58% since its last report, now standing at 3.96% of tradable shares. This reduction in short interest suggests a more bullish sentiment among investors towards the company. Compared to its peers, Starbucks has a lower short interest, with the peer group average being 11.45%.

Starbucks Objects to Burn Plaintiff’s Requests for Financial Info. Starbucks is asking a Los Angeles judge to block requests for financial information made by a customer, Sala Lily Shabdiz, who is suing the company for severe burns allegedly sustained from a spilled hot drink. The coffee giant argues that Shabdiz's requests for detailed financial analysis, settlement history related to burn injuries, and profit/loss statements are premature and burdensome, as her medical specials are less than $3,000. Shabdiz, a USC student, claims she suffered severe thigh burns in February 2022 when the lid of her over 200-degree tea came off due to the way the cup was handed to her in the drive-through.

Starbucks terminates employee over pig drawing incident involving LA County deputy. Starbucks has fired a barista who gave a Los Angeles County sheriff’s deputy a coffee cup with a pig drawn on it. The company stated that the drawing, based on an internet meme, was intended for internal staff and not for customers, and immediately launched an investigation after the incident. Starbucks has apologized to the deputy and law enforcement, emphasizing its respect for police and commitment to ensuring all customers feel welcome.

Starbucks Objects to Burn Plaintiff’s Requests for Company Financial Info. Starbucks is asking a Los Angeles judge to block requests for company financial information made by a customer suing the coffee chain for severe burns from a spilled hot drink. The plaintiff, Sala Lily Shabdiz, seeks punitive damages and extensive financial documents, including analyses of redesigning hot beverage packaging and past burn-related settlement figures, which Starbucks deems premature and unduly burdensome. Shabdiz, a former USC student, claims she suffered severe thigh burns in February 2022 when a hot tea lid came off due to improper handling and packaging.
DIS: Thailand Ready to Build Asia's Fifth Disneyland. Thailand is proposing to build a Disneyland in its Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC) to attract high-spending international tourists. This would be Asia's fifth Disneyland, following locations in Japan, Hong Kong, and China. The project is estimated to be worth nearly Rp54 trillion and would likely operate under a licensing structure, with a Thai investor responsible for development and The Walt Disney Company retaining intellectual property rights and receiving royalty fees.

Disney Removes Controversial CEO, Erased From Company Records. The Walt Disney Company is reportedly removing former CEO Bob Chapek from its official corporate timeline on its website, effectively erasing him from company records. Chapek, who served from 2020 to 2022, was a controversial figure, known for price increases at theme parks, a dispute with Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, and a "quantity-over-quality" content strategy for Disney+. His tenure ended abruptly when Bob Iger returned as CEO, and Disney has not issued a statement regarding the removal of the historical timeline from its website.

The Walt Disney Company – Streaming turns profitable. Phillip Securities Research has initiated coverage of The Walt Disney Company with an "ACCUMULATE" rating and a target price of US$130.00. The report highlights Disney's diversified portfolio, strong intellectual property, and the successful profitability of its direct-to-consumer (DTC) streaming segment in 2H24. The Experiences segment is identified as the primary growth driver, showing consistent revenue growth and supported by resilient attendance.
